dataNotSaved.msg: trying to break data into parts
trying to break data into parts

11.response: {"Q0":"Probably something along the lines of seeing how people make choices when real money is being offered. Like if someone is more likely to go for the safe option (a high probability galaxy) over a less safe one just because the chance of earning money is higher."}

0.response: {"Q0":"Not really, I just looked at the screen and always did planets with gems which could be sold. Other than that I just chose the galaxy which felt right when comparing differences in cost vs chance. Also I ignored the green rocks during the meteor showers."}

7.response: {"Q0":"It didn't impact my motivation at all, no. I just treated the game the exact same way."}

9.response: {"Q0":"I was more focused on the galaxy part because of the 1 second timer when having to make the choice. You've got a lot of time to excavate a planet to the point where I didn't build much muscle memory for those sections, I basically had to look at what I had to dig through and confirm which tool was what a lot of the time. Also I had to focus a lot during the meteor showers of course!"}

8.response: {"Q0":"If the gap wasn't big value wise (like a $1 difference) then I'd usually pick the one with a higher chance. If it was a big gap then I'd usually go for the higher value one unless it was like 10% vs 70%. Of course sometimes I felt a bit different during the moment but this was my general approach."}

4.response: {"Q0":"Yes, gems of the associated planet become worthless after they leave."}
